probable_causes: Moisture ingress through deteriorated silica gel breather
Oxidation of oil due to aging and thermal stress
Cellulose insulation degradation releasing dissolved gases
External contamination through leaking gaskets or seals
diagnostic_procedures: Perform dissolved gas analysis (DGA) — key gases: H2, CH4, C2H2, C2H4, CO
Test oil BDV (breakdown voltage), moisture content, acidity, IFT
Check furan content for cellulose insulation aging assessment
Inspect silica gel breather — color change indicates moisture saturation
corrective_actions: Filter and dehydrate transformer oil using online filtration plant
Replace oil charge if acidity or moisture beyond recoverable limits
Replace deteriorated silica gel in breather
Repair gasket leaks at flanges and valve joints
preventive_actions: Schedule DGA testing quarterly for critical transformers
Maintain silica gel breather in good condition — replace when saturated
Monitor oil temperature and loading to prevent accelerated aging
Install online DGA monitoring for important transformers
safety_precautions: Transformer oil is combustible — maintain fire protection systems
Use proper PPE when handling oil samples
De-energize and isolate transformer before any oil system maintenance

probable_causes: Moisture ingress into bushing condenser core
Partial discharge due to internal voids or contamination
External flashover from pollution or rain on bushing surface
diagnostic_procedures: Measure bushing tan-delta and capacitance values
Inspect bushing surface for cracks, chipping, or tracking marks
Check oil level in oil-filled bushings
corrective_actions: Replace bushing if tan-delta exceeds limits
Clean bushing external surface with approved cleaning agents
Top up oil in oil-filled bushings if low
Replace gaskets if oil leakage observed at bushing flange
preventive_actions: Include bushing tan-delta test in annual maintenance schedule
Keep bushing surfaces clean — wash during planned outages
Monitor bushing oil level through sight glass regularly
safety_precautions: Bushing failure can cause catastrophic explosion — maintain safe distance during testing
Always de-energize and ground transformer before bushing work

probable_causes: Contact wear or pitting from arcing during tap change
OLTC oil contamination with carbon deposits
diagnostic_procedures: Analyze OLTC oil separately — DGA and particle count
Check contact resistance across all tap positions
Verify tap changer operation cycle time and sequence
Inspect mechanism drive linkages for wear
corrective_actions: Overhaul OLTC — replace worn contacts and springs
Realign drive mechanism and adjust timing
Replace diverter switch if arcing damage is extensive
preventive_actions: Schedule OLTC overhaul based on number of tap operations (typically 50,000-100,000)
Test OLTC oil quality separately from main tank oil
Record and trend tap operation counter and motor current
safety_precautions: Isolate OLTC motor supply before maintenance
OLTC compartment may contain gases — ventilate before entry
